front: What are the three time semantics in Flink — event time, processing time, and ingestion time — and which should you use?
back: Event time is the timestamp embedded in the event itself (e.g., when a click actually happened). It produces deterministic, reproducible results regardless of when data arrives or how fast Flink processes it — essential for correct business analytics. Processing time is the wall-clock time when Flink processes the event. It's the simplest and lowest-latency option but produces non-deterministic results since results change based on system speed. Ingestion time is when the event enters Flink's source operator — a middle ground, but rarely used in practice. Best practice: always use event time for business-critical pipelines. Use processing time only for approximate, latency-sensitive use cases where correctness is secondary (e.g., real-time dashboards with best-effort accuracy).
